title: Coin Flip Simulator with Global Engine
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program that uses a file-scope `std::mt19937` engine to simulate N coin flips and print `Heads` or `Tails` for each.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10).

## Output
- Print exactly N lines, each containing either `Heads` or `Tails` (capital first letter, no extra text).
- Use a global `std::mt19937 rng{ 42u }` defined at file scope (outside `main`).

## Requirements
1. Declare `std::mt19937 rng{ 42u }` at file scope (outside `main`).
2. Use `std::uniform_int_distribution<int>{0, 1}` where 0 means `Heads` and 1 means `Tails`.
3. The engine must be created only once and never re-seeded inside the loop.
4. Print `Heads` or `Tails` (exact spelling) on separate lines.

## Notes
- `#include <random>` and `#include <iostream>` are required.

test_cases.label: Hidden: 6 flips
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Heads
Tails
Heads
Heads
Heads
Tails

title: Fix the Re-Seeding Bug
prompt_md:
## Goal
The program below is supposed to print N different random integers in [1, 10] using a global `std::mt19937` engine. It compiles and runs, but all outputs are the same number. Find and fix the bug.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10).

## Output
- Print exactly N lines, each with a different random integer in [1, 10].
- Use `std::mt19937 rng{ 42u }` at file scope.

## Requirements
1. The engine must be seeded exactly once, before the loop runs.
2. Each of the N outputs must come from a fresh draw from the engine (not a repeated first draw).
3. Do not change the seed value or the distribution range.

## Notes
- The bug is a logic error inside the loop — one line needs to be removed.
